titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to William J.H. Tyrrell 26, September, 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Written during William Upton Tyrrell's journey back to the front after leave.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Royal Pavilion Hotel, Folkestone, Kent, England
Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
THE-FREDERICK-HOTELS-LIMITED. HOTEL GREAT CENTRAL, LONDON
& WHARNCLIFFE ROOMS,
HOTEL RUSSELL, LONDON.
ROYAL PAVILION HOTEL, FOLKESTONE. HOTEL MAJESTIC, HARROGATE.
HOTEL BURLINGTON IN DOVER BAY.
SACKVILLE HOTEL, BEXHILL ON SEA.
HOTEL MOETROPLE, WHITBY. HOTEL BRISTOL, BEAULIEU-SUR-MER Telegraphic Address,
PAVILION, FOLKESTONE. Telephone,
No 86, FOLKESTONE. ROYAL PAVILION HOTEL,
FOLKESTONE, 26 Sept 1917 My dear Daddy, I hope you got home
safe and dry last night.
I have got this far and
have a couple of hours to
wait before the boat leaves. We started very late from
Kingstown and had just
about 5 minutes to spare in London
Mr B was on the platform
when I got down last night
so I had a long chat with
him. I think it is quite correct
about the 2nd Bus going to
Ulster Dis. In that case we
will be near Bafourne I
will be able to let you know
somehow all about it. No news Best love Your most affec Son Willie
